text: Retraction of the lung laterally demonstrates the superior pulmonary vein with associated hilar lymph nodes that are being dissected in this first scene.
Dissection of the hilar lymph nodes and mobilization of the vascular structures allow for division of these perfused structures with the vascular stapler.
We can identify additional hilar lymph nodes along with the left upper lobe bronchus as well as the pulmonary artery branches to the left upper lobe posterior to the vein.
They can be divided using a vascular stapler.

This simulation model is completely perfused both from an arterial and venous standpoint attempting to maintain physiologic pressure as demonstrated by entry into a pulmonary artery branch during this dissection.
